content.problemStatement:
Chef recently read about the mex function and came up with a function similar to that. For a (possibly empty) sequence of positive integers SSS, Chef defines f(S)f(S)f(S) as the smallest positive integer that does not appear in SSS. For example, f([])=1f([])=1f([])=1, f([3,4,3,5])=1f([3,4,3,5])=1f([3,4,3,5])=1, f([2,5,1,1,2,3])=4f([2,5,1,1,2,3])=4f([2,5,1,1,2,3])=4.

Chef's friend heard about Chef's idea and found it really cool. Therefore, he sent a sequence a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äã to Chef and asked him to find the sum of f(S)f(S)f(S) over all 2N2^N2N possible subsequences SSS of this sequence. Since Chef is busy, he entrusted you with solving the problem.

Since the resulting sum can be very big, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the sum of f(S)f(S)f(S) over all subsequences modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
